critic.verdict: An absolute masterclass in Easy chart design, featuring flawless rhythm sync, extremely comfortable cradle-flow streams, and smart, structured difficulty progression.

issues.problem: Continuous 26-note stream of 1/4 notes is technically long for an Easy chart, though the low BPM (137) and repeating, highly comfortable 'cradle' pattern (R-U-D, L-U-D) make it exceptionally accessible and fun to play.

critic.verdict: Great rhythm and flow are completely undermined by a repeating error where jumps are placed during holds, forcing illegal 3-panel 'hands' on a Medium single chart.

issues.problem: While holding L (38.084 to 38.960), the player has to hit a D -> U 16th-note transition with the right foot, which is an extremely awkward vertical slide for this difficulty.
JUMP DR is placed during an active HOLD L (56.809 to 57.685), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (L, D, R).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (62.175 to 63.051), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).
JUMP LU is placed during an active HOLD D (84.951 to 85.827), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, U).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (86.703 to 87.579), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).
JUMP LU is placed during an active HOLD D (90.207 to 91.083), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, U).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (95.463 to 97.215), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (95.463 to 97.215), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (98.967 to 99.843), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD U (102.361 to 103.237), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (U, L, R).
JUMP LR is placed during an active HOLD D (104.223 to 105.099), forcing an illegal 3-panel 'hand' (D, L, R).

critic.verdict: The chart is extremely repetitive, lazily mapped, and contains a major mechanical error requiring three feet to play during a hold.

issues.problem: While HOLD D is active, the chart features JUMP LR at 95.901 and 96.777. Holding D while jumping on L and R simultaneously is physically impossible on a standard pad without using hands or brackets, which is a major design oversight.

The entire chorus is mapped to an extremely repetitive alternating pattern of JUMP LR and tap D on almost every beat. This completely ignores the syncopated vocal melody and feels incredibly tedious to play.
The second chorus repeats the exact same lazy pattern of LR/LU/DR jumps and D taps, showing a lack of creative mapping and ignoring the musical structure.
The transition from JUMP LU -> tap D -> 16th tap R -> JUMP LU requires an extremely awkward and rushed foot transfer on a 16th note, leading to poor physical flow.
